overview: This step leverages existing translations into extracted text units by querying translation resources (TM engines or MT systems) and optionally copying the best match into the target content. It processes filter events, skipping non-translatable and approved text units. Multiple Leveraging steps can be chained to query different resources in sequence — e.g., a TM first, then an MT engine for unleveraged entries.

leverage.help: Master switch for this step. Allows you to keep the step in your pipeline configuration while toggling it on and off without removing it.

resourceClassName.help:
Fully qualified Java class name of the translation resource connector to use. Can be a TM connector or an MT engine connector.

For available connectors and their configuration, see the **Connectors** documentation.
resourceClassName.values:
Fully qualified Java class name. Common values include:
- `net.sf.okapi.connectors.pensieve.PensieveTMConnector` — Pensieve TM
- Various MT engine connectors depending on installation

noQueryThreshold.help:
If a text unit already has a translation candidate with a score at or above this value, no query is sent to the translation resource. This is useful for avoiding queries to **for-fee resources** (e.g., MT engines) when a good-enough match already exists from a previous leveraging step.

Set to **101** to always query regardless of existing candidates.
noQueryThreshold.values: Integer score threshold (0–101). Use `101` to always allow the query.
examples: `100` — skip query if an exact match already exists
`95` — skip query if a 95%+ match already exists

threshold.help: Minimum match score for a result to be accepted. Matches below this score are discarded entirely and not added to the candidate list.

fillTarget.help: Copies the translation of the best match directly into the target content of the text unit, provided its score meets the `fillTargetThreshold`. If the score is below the threshold, the match is still added to the candidate list but **not** copied into the target.

fillTargetThreshold.help: The minimum score the best match must have for its translation to be copied into the target content. Matches below this threshold remain in the candidate list but are not applied to the target.

dependsOn.condition: must be true for this threshold to have any effect

fillIfTargetIsEmpty.help: Restricts target filling to only text units where the target is currently empty. Prevents overwriting existing translations.

fillIfTargetIsSameAsSource.help: Also fills the target when the existing target content is identical (text and codes) to the source. This catches cases where the target was pre-populated with a copy of the source rather than a real translation.

dependsOn.condition: must be true — this option extends the 'only if empty' behavior

downgradeIdenticalBestMatches.help: Downgrades by **1%** the score of top exact matches that are identical to each other. When a connector returns multiple exact matches with different translations, some downstream components may trigger automated actions on exact matches. This option prevents such automation when the "exact match" is ambiguous.
notes: Only affects scoring when multiple identical best matches exist — a single unique exact match is not downgraded.

makeTMX.help: Creates a single TMX document containing all entries for which a match was found across all input files. Useful for archiving leveraged results or feeding them into another TM.

tmxPath.help: Full file path for the generated TMX document. Supports pipeline variables for dynamic paths.
tmxPath.values:
File path string. Supports variables:
- `${rootDir}` — pipeline root directory
- `${inputRootDir}` — input root directory
- `${srcLoc}`, `${trgLoc}` — source/target locale codes
- Other locale variables

useMTPrefix.help: Adds an MT-specific prefix to the source text for entries coming from an MT engine. This ensures the entry will only be treated as a **fuzzy match** in tools that cannot identify MT origin via the `creationid` attribute.
notes: This is important for downstream TM tools that may otherwise treat MT output as an exact human translation.

useTargetPrefix.help: Adds a configurable prefix to the front of the leveraged translation text when a match is found and copied into the target. The prefix is only added when the match score is at or below `targetPrefixThreshold`.

targetPrefix.help: The prefix string prepended to leveraged translations when `useTargetPrefix` is enabled and the match score is at or below the threshold.
examples: `FUZZY__` — default prefix to flag fuzzy matches

targetPrefixThreshold.help: The prefix is added to the translation only when the best match score is **equal to or below** this value. Matches above this threshold are copied without the prefix.
targetPrefixThreshold.values: Integer percentage (0–100). Set to `99` to prefix all fuzzy matches but not exact (100%) matches.

copySourceOnNoText.help: Copies the source content into the target when the source contains **no translatable text** (only whitespace and/or inline codes). This overwrites any existing target content for such entries.

processingNotes: Text units flagged as **non-translatable** are skipped entirely.
Text units with the `approved` property set to `yes` are skipped — they are assumed to already have a valid translation.
When text units are segmented, queries are sent per-segment rather than per-text-unit.
Matches are stored as annotations attached to the target container or target segments of the text unit.
Multiple Leveraging steps can be chained in a pipeline to query different resources in sequence (e.g., TM first, then MT for remaining entries).
Internet-based translation resources (cloud TM/MT) may result in lengthy processing times.

examples.title: Chain TM then MT leveraging
examples.description: Use two Leveraging steps: the first queries a local TM, the second queries an MT engine only for entries that didn't get a high-quality TM match. Set `noQueryThreshold` on the second step to skip entries already matched by the TM.
